file.content:
## 4. Skill family

`.claude/rules/skill-authoring.md` mandates that operational artefacts
reference specs rather than duplicating them. Every skill below points at
this spec and at `docs/VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md`, and includes the
conflict-resolution clause.

Common shape every skill follows:

1. Argument: an issue number (or `--vendor <slug>` for `reverify`).
2. Read durable state: `gh issue view`, `gh pr view` (when a PR is linked),
   the contract spec, the vendor doc.
3. Verify the current `phase:*` label matches what the skill expects. If
   it doesn't, refuse and tell the user which skill should run instead.
4. Perform the work.
5. Apply the next `phase:*` label (or, for skills that don't transition,
   post a tally / report comment).
6. Hand off explicitly: name the next skill the user should invoke when
   the next event happens.

Skills never poll, never run in the background. They run when the
maintainer invokes them in response to an event.

| Skill | Phases owned | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `assistant` | (router) | Reads `type:*` + `phase:*`; routes to the matching sub-skill. Stops; does not perform work itself. |
| `assistant-triage` | 3.1.2 | Optional helper. Applies `kind:*` for vendor-evolution. Drafts decision comment. |
| `assistant-implement` | 3.1.3 → 3.1.4 readiness | Branches on `type:*`. Scaffolds (new-assistant) or refactors (vendor-evolution). |
| `assistant-review` | 3.1.4 | Documentation-first review. Refuses to apply `phase:testing` itself. |
| `assistant-tester-followup` | 3.1.5 | Tally + log audit. Knows the `kind:urgent-fix` threshold escape. Does not transition. |
| `assistant-merge` | 3.1.6 → 3.1.7 | Re-verifies gates. Squash-merges. Applies `phase:merged`. |
| `assistant-release` | 3.1.8 | Branches on `type:*` and `kind:*` for README / release-notes treatment. |
| `assistant-reverify` | (decoupled) | Doc-only refresh PR or files a `type:vendor-evolution` issue. |

## 5. Issue forms, PR template, gating action

2. Read durable state: `gh issue view`, `gh pr view` (when a PR is linked),
   the contract spec, the vendor doc.
3. Verify the current `phase:*` label matches what the skill expects. If
   it doesn't, refuse and tell the user which skill should run instead.
4. Perform the work.
5. Apply the next `phase:*` label (or, for skills that don't transition,
   post a tally / report comment).
6. Hand off explicitly: name the next skill the user should invoke when
   the next event happens.
input.new_string:
Common shape every skill follows (also see
`.claude/rules/skill-handoff.md` for the shared execution pattern):

1. Argument: an issue number (or `--vendor <slug>` for `reverify`, or
   a free-form intent for `propose`).
2. Read durable state: `gh issue view`, `gh pr view` (when a PR is
   linked), the contract spec, the vendor doc. Apply the Phase-A
   self-check (mutex auto-correction).
3. Verify the current `phase:*` label matches what the skill expects.
   If it doesn't, refuse and tell the user which skill should run
   instead.
4. Perform the work.
5. Propose the next `phase:*` transition (or the relevant `gh`
   command) and execute it after a single Y/n confirmation. The
   maintainer owns the decision; the skill owns the mechanical
   execution.
6. Hand off explicitly: name the next skill the user should invoke
   when the next event happens.

input.new_string:
| `assistant` | (router) | Reads `type:*` + `phase:*`; routes to the matching sub-skill. Phase 0 bootstrap-labels check. Stops; does not perform work itself. |
| `assistant-propose` | 3.1.1 (intent → issue) | Optional. Classifies a free-form intent, pre-fills the issue body, files via `gh issue create`. |
| `assistant-triage` | 3.1.2 | Optional helper. Applies `kind:*` for vendor-evolution. Enforces the same existence check as `assistant-implement`. Drafts decision comment. |
| `assistant-implement` | 3.1.3 → 3.1.4 readiness | Branches on `type:*`. Scaffolds (new-assistant) or refactors (vendor-evolution). |
| `assistant-review` | 3.1.4 | Documentation-first review. PR pre-flight (draft, mergeable, author-vs-reviewer). Proposes the `phase:testing` transition for a single Y/n confirmation. |
| `assistant-tester-followup` | 3.1.5 | Tally + log audit. Knows the `kind:urgent-fix` threshold escape. Proposes `phase:merge-ready` for a single Y/n confirmation once the gate is green. |
| `assistant-merge` | 3.1.6 → 3.1.7 | Re-verifies gates. Commits README / index updates to the PR branch and embeds the release-notes draft in the squash-merge commit body. Applies `phase:merged`. |
| `assistant-release` | 3.1.8 | Copies the squash-merge commit body into the GitHub release notes once a tag ships. |
| `assistant-reverify` | (decoupled) | Doc-only refresh PR or files a `type:vendor-evolution` issue. |

+| `assistant-release` | 3.1.8 | Copies the squash-merge commit body into the GitHub release notes once a tag ships. |
| `assistant-reverify` | (decoupled) | Doc-only refresh PR or files a `type:vendor-evolution` issue. |
## 5. Issue forms, PR template, gating action

content.text:
# Smart commit

Tu dois committer les changements git en cours dans le repo courant en suivant les guidelines ci-dessous.

## Contexte utilisateur

Intent fourni par l'utilisateur (peut être vide) : `avec des commits les plus atomiques possible`

## Étapes

### 1. Analyser l'état du repo

Lance en parallèle :
- `git status` (sans `-uall`)
- `git diff --staged` et `git diff` pour voir les changements
- `git log -n 5 --pretty=format:'%h %s%n%b%n---'` pour voir les 4-5 derniers commits

### 2. Détecter les conventions du projet

À partir des 4-5 derniers commits :
- **Conventional commits ?** Si la majorité suit le format `type(scope): message` (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, build, ci...) → utilise ce format. Sinon → format libre.
- **Langue ?** Détecte la langue dominante des messages (français, anglais, autre) → utilise la même langue. En cas de doute, français.
- **Style ?** Impératif vs descriptif, majuscule initiale ou non, présence de scope, etc. → imite.

### 3. Détecter un éventuel fixup

Si l'intent utilisateur mentionne un correctif (mots-clés : "fix", "corrige", "répare", "bug", "issue", "problème", "régression", "broken"...) :

1. Identifie les fichiers modifiés (`git diff --name-only HEAD` + fichiers staged).
2. Pour chaque fichier, regarde `git log -n 10 --oneline -- <fichier>` pour voir quels commits récents l'ont touché.
3. Si **un seul commit récent** (dans les ~5 derniers, non encore pushé sur main/master partagé — vérifie avec `git log @{u}..HEAD` si upstream) est clairement à l'origine du code cassé, propose un **`git commit --fixup=<sha>`** au lieu d'un commit classique.
4. Si ambigu ou si le commit date d'avant `@{u}` (déjà pushé en amont partagé), reste sur un commit classique.

Ne fais **pas** de rebase autosquash automatiquement — laisse ce soin à l'utilisateur.

### 4. Rédiger le message

Règles impératives :
- **Le message explique le POURQUOI**, pas le QUOI. Le diff dit déjà ce qui change. Le message dit pourquoi ce changement existe (motivation, contrainte, problème résolu, contexte métier).
- Sujet court (≤72 caractères), en suivant la convention détectée.
- Si le pourquoi ne tient pas dans le sujet, ajoute un corps (ligne vide + explication). Sinon, pas de corps inutile.
- Pas de name-dropping de fichiers/fonctions dans le sujet — le diff suffit.
- Pas de phrases vides genre « Mise à jour du code » ou « Divers fixes ».

Si l'intent utilisateur est vide ou trop vague pour déduire le pourquoi, et que le diff ne le rend pas évident, **demande à l'utilisateur** avant de committer plutôt que d'inventer.

### 5. Staging et commit

- Si rien n'est staged, stage les fichiers pertinents **par nom** (jamais `git add -A` ni `git add .` pour éviter d'embarquer secrets/binaires).
- Ignore les fichiers suspects (`.env`, `credentials*`, clés privées, dumps volumineux). Préviens l'utilisateur si tu en vois.
- Crée le commit via HEREDOC pour préserver le formatage multiligne.
- **Ne jamais** utiliser `--no-verify`, `--amend` (sauf demande explicite), ni `--no-gpg-sign`.
- Si un hook pre-commit échoue : diagnostique la cause, corrige, re-stage, et crée un **nouveau** commit (pas d'amend).

### 6. Vérification finale

Après commit, lance `git status` et `git log -1` pour confirmer. Reporte brièvement : sha court + sujet + (fixup le cas échéant).

## Garde-fous

- Ne push jamais.
- Ne crée pas de commit vide.
- Ne touche pas à `git config`.
- Si plusieurs "unités logiques" distinctes sont mélangées dans le diff, préviens l'utilisateur et propose de splitter plutôt que de tout entasser. Tu peux utiliser `git stash` (avec `-u`/`--keep-index` si pertinent) pour isoler des sous-ensembles — mais cette commande n'est **pas** auto-approuvée, l'utilisateur devra confirmer à chaque usage : ne l'utilise que si c'est vraiment nécessaire.

content.content:
ede1e02 docs(assistant): allow tester confirmations to span multiple builds
Relax the build SHA validation in the counting rules. Confirmations on
prior build SHAs remain valid for the threshold calculation as long as
the attached log audits clean against the vendor doc's Sanitized fields.

The build SHA is still required for traceability (the follow-up skill
records which SHA each tester exercised) but is no longer a blocker to
merging. This accommodates testing workflows where the PR receives
multiple updates between tester sign-offs without forcing each tester to
re-verify on the latest build.

Update the spec (docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md), the operational skill
(.claude/skills/assistant-tester-followup/SKILL.md), and correct the
skill description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 2c5b4b3bebcb

---
79e6ed8 docs(assistant): allow PR author to sign off as a tester
Remove the "non-author tester" restriction. The PR author can now post a valid
✅ tester-confirm sign-off and have it counted toward the merge threshold (≥2
distinct testers for new-assistant/enrichment/breaking, ≥1 for urgent-fix).

The author exercises the same testing workflow as any other tester — building
the DMG, running it against their vendor account, and verifying correctness.
The only difference is that they also authored the code. Nothing in the sign-off
template or audit discipline changes; the change is purely in the counting rule.

Update the spec (`docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md`), the operational artifacts
(assistant-tester-followup and assistant-merge skills), the review checklist,
issue templates, and README.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 35fd8933f197

---
08790be ci(assistant-build): show build timestamp in sticky comment
The sticky is rewritten on every commit pushed to the PR, so testers had no way
to tell whether the comment they were reading was 5 minutes or 5 days old —
which matters because they pin the build SHA in their sign-off and may rebuild
locally if the DMG is stale.

Add an ISO 8601 UTC timestamp (minute precision) right under the heading so the
freshness signal is unambiguous at a glance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 539e3b9e1209

---
b757a6e ci(assistant-build): surface build-from-source alternative next to the download
The build-from-source link was buried at the bottom of the sticky and framed
defensively ("Don't trust this DMG?"), making it look like a fallback for
paranoid testers rather than a first-class option. Move it directly under the
download bullet and reword neutrally so testers see both paths side by side
and pick whichever fits their setup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: bb2c889407ee

---
0f74218 fixup! fix: improve tester feedback UX and preserve account state on transient errors
Entire-Checkpoint: 95155bcbea56

---

Je vais créer 7 commits atomiques, un par unité logique. Regardons les modifications par groupe.

**Commits planifiés :**

1. **Shared rule** : `.claude/rules/skill-handoff.md`
2. **New skill propose** : `.claude/skills/assistant-propose/SKILL.md`
3. **Router bootstrap & propose routing** : `assistant/SKILL.md`
4. **Spec updates for propose** : `docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md` §3.1.1 + §4
5. **Doc existence convergence** : `assistant-triage/SKILL.md` + `assistant-implement/SKILL.md`
6. **Phase-A checks & pre-flight** : `assistant-implement/SKILL.md` Phase A + `assistant-review/SKILL.md` + `assistant-merge/SKILL.md` Phase A + handoff rule references
7. **Tester-followup re-run & release pre-move** : `assistant-tester-followup/SKILL.md` + `assistant-merge/SKILL.md` phases C–G + `assistant-release/SKILL.md` + `docs/ASSISTANT-ONBOARDING.md` §3.1.6, 3.1.8

git add .claude/rules/skill-handoff.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(.claude/rules): add shared skill-handoff execution pattern

Define the common HITL execution pattern across all assistant-* skills:
Phase-A self-check for label drift (mutex auto-correction), PR pre-flight
checks (draft/mergeable/author-vs-reviewer), and single Y/n confirmation
for mechanical transitions. Replaces ad-hoc per-skill patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git add .claude/skills/assistant-propose/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(.claude/skills/assistant-propose): new skill to classify intent and file issues

Add a new entry point for the assistant workflow. When the user describes
what they want to support or fix in free-form language (rather than opening
an issue manually), this skill classifies the intent (new-assistant vs
vendor-evolution), pre-fills the issue body from existing context
(docs/vendors/<slug>.md, the user's message), and creates the issue via
gh issue create with the correct labels.

Complements the issue forms for users who prefer describing intent in chat
without copying fields into the UI. Eliminates the discovery friction of
"where do I find the issue form?".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git add .claude/skills/assistant/SKILL.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(.claude/skills/assistant): add Phase 0 bootstrap check and route to propose

Add a Phase 0 bootstrap check that verifies the required GitHub labels
exist on the repo (type:*, kind:*, phase:*). If missing, propose to run
scripts/bootstrap-onboarding-labels.sh with a single Y/n confirmation.

Extend the router to handle the case where no issue number is provided —
this signals the user wants to describe an intent in free-form. Route to
assistant-propose.

Update the phase-to-skill routing table to include the new propose entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

    feat(.claude/skills/assistant-merge,release): move user-facing artefacts and release notes to pre-merge
    
    Restructure the merge and release workflow to front-load user-facing updates.
    
    In assistant-merge:
    - New Phase C commits README and docs/vendors/index.md updates to the PR
      branch (new-assistant only; vendor-evolution has no README change).
    - New Phase D drafts the release-notes text (testers credited, BREAKING
      context, urgent-fix context) for user sign-off.
    - New Phase E embeds the release-notes draft in the squash-merge commit
      **body** (not the title), making it the single source of truth.
    - Renumber subsequent phases (F, G).
    
    In assistant-release:
    - Simplify to Phase B: copy the squash-merge commit body into
      gh release edit (no regeneration of README or notes).
    - Remove Phase B's artefact rewrites and Phase C's release-notes generation.
    - All README and notes updates now ship in the merge commit.
    
    This eliminates the post-tag editing friction and ensures README updates
    land in the same commit as the code (rather than days later when the tag
    exists).
